title: The People Are on Our Side; Victory Is Ours!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The source contains deliberate boxed repetitions of the Coimbatore election-official passage, the appeal not to be shaken by alleged cash distribution, and the final call to work because the people are on the alliance's side. They are retained in their printed positions. The scan also restores the terminal date as `13.5.2016`, which the OCR rendered as `137522016`. The Tamil reading copy below repairs only such unambiguous source readings; the canonical Tamil source remains unchanged. Allegations concerning cash distribution and official conduct remain attributed to the reports and claims presented in the letter.
paragraphs: If one opens Tamil Nadu's newspapers over the past few days, the reports arriving without pause could make one wonder whether Tamil Nadu under Jayalalithaa has become a State overflowing with money: so many lakhs seized here, so many crores seized there, money distributed to voters without hindrance, electricity switched off so that cash can be handed out under cover of darkness. At every election, the AIADMK discovers a new method of distributing money and thereby “enriches” Indian democratic elections!

During the 2014 parliamentary election, they joined hands with the Election Commission, imposed prohibitory orders under Section 144 at the last moment, and allegedly used them to distribute money with police assistance. In this election, they have reportedly cut the electricity supply and used the darkness to distribute money in many places.

Reports say that the ruling AIADMK, having finally realised that it cannot overcome the voters' dissatisfaction and anger and win again, is throwing about bundles of money it had plundered. At election time, no other State has seen seizures on this scale, amounting to nearly Rs 100 crore. Nor is this confined to cash distribution. Eggs filled with acid were thrown at Ambattur Congress candidate Hasan Maulana and those campaigning with him, and the candidate is now receiving treatment in hospital. Were these not the people who carried out the acid attack on IAS officer Chandralekha? It appears they continue the same habit!

In Coimbatore district, two officials, the returning officer for Coimbatore South and the returning officer for Thondamuthur, forgot their lawful duty and conscience and allegedly acted directly in support of ruling-party candidates. It became known that they had even helped an AIADMK candidate transport money in government vehicles. Only after the money had been moved and everything was over did the Election Commission finally awaken, pretend that it was acting impartially, and seize the officials' cars.

In Coimbatore district, two officials, the returning officer for Coimbatore South and the returning officer for Thondamuthur, forgot their lawful duty and conscience and allegedly acted directly in support of ruling-party candidates. It became known that they had even helped an AIADMK candidate transport money in government vehicles. Only after the money had been moved and everything was over did the Election Commission finally awaken, pretend that it was acting impartially, and seize the officials' cars.

What these events tell us is that fear of defeat and despair have now taken hold of the ruling party. That is why they have begun engaging in such dishonourable acts. With only two days left before the election, ministers and members of the ruling party are daydreaming that they can scatter part of the money they allegedly plundered and hoarded, dazzle the voters and somehow win. If money alone determined elections, and if only millionaires and billionaires could win, would not the Tatas, Birlas, Ambanis and Adanis be occupying the seat of government in India?

Kazhagam comrades, and especially Kazhagam candidates, need not be troubled in the slightest by such lawless excesses of the AIADMK, which scatters money in an attempt to ruin a free and fair election.

Kazhagam comrades, and especially Kazhagam candidates, need not be troubled in the slightest by such lawless excesses of the AIADMK, which scatters money in an attempt to ruin a free and fair election. Did we not know, even before election work began, that the ruling party would use in this election the crores it had accumulated over five years? Was it not known to all that, calculating Rs 5 crore for each constituency, they had allegedly moved and hidden more than Rs 1,000 crore across the State with police assistance, using police vehicles and ambulances? Pay no heed to the money the ruling party has distributed. I lovingly ask you to continue working without pause for the victory of the DMK alliance, which has laboured tirelessly for Tamil, the Tamil people and the welfare of Tamil Nadu.

I am ninety-two years old. Even at this age, I have come here to Tiruvarur to meet you in person and ask you to vote for the Rising Sun. We are people who know the great truth that the voice of the people is the voice of God.

Unlike some, I did not fly here by helicopter to campaign. I do not know how to gather voters at a single place convenient to me, make them stand in the scorching sun and ask for their votes. My custom is to seek out voters and go to where they are. Hundreds of miles by road in the campaign vehicle, travel by train and travel on ordinary passenger aircraft: I have made no special travel arrangements for this campaign. Because I am a very ordinary, common man, the thought of extravagance does not arise in me as it does in some others.

Nor have I come before the people asking for votes by repeating stale falsehoods like some do: “I have bent the sky into a bow; I have twisted sand into rope; spring is blowing across Tamil Nadu; if you vote for me, I shall raise the people to unimaginable heights.”

The AIADMK did not fulfil many promises in its 2011 election manifesto. Nor did it implement many schemes announced under Rule 110 of the Assembly. All of you know this! I am one who does what I say. Once I have said something, I work intensely until it is done. Unlike some, I have never said whatever came to my lips and continually deceived the voters and the people of Tamil Nadu.

It is not I alone. At the age of ninety-three, our Kazhagam General Secretary, the Professor, completed the first and second stages of his tour and is now campaigning enthusiastically in its third stage.

There is no need even to speak about the Kazhagam treasurer! I once described him in these words: “Work! Work! Work! That is Kazhagam treasurer Thalapathi M.K. Stalin.” I saw on television that he has become the living expression of that definition, engaging in intense campaign work even during his morning walk.

Pay not the slightest heed to the money scattered by the AIADMK, the garden of cash they have allegedly plundered and kept at hand, their deceitful schemes, or the Election Commission's inaction as it stands with folded arms and watches everything. The people are on our side. Therefore, let us work; let us work tirelessly; whatever the obstacle, let us cast it aside and work with smiling faces. Victory is ours!
You will also have seen reports of Rajya Sabha DMK group leader Kanimozhi and all the Headquarters speakers continuing their campaign work and journeys without regard to night or day.

Only two days now stand between us and the election. That is just forty-eight hours! Must not all the work done thus far bear its proper fruit? With the right born of our relationship, I ask each of you to labour to bring dawn to the Tamil people after all the suffering they have endured over the past five years.
Thiruvalluvar's precious Kural says:
> “Even fate will reveal its back to those > who strive without faltering and never give way.”

Therefore, pay not the slightest heed to the money scattered by the AIADMK, the garden of cash they have allegedly plundered and kept at hand, their deceitful schemes, or the Election Commission's inaction as it stands with folded arms and watches everything. The people are on our side. Let us work; let us work tirelessly; whatever the obstacle, let us cast it aside and work with smiling faces. Victory is ours!
With affection,
M.K.
